screenEvents.detail: Fleet size and demo follow up notes
Line item pricing side by side

transcript.speaker: Omar
transcript.text: Tessa, you saw the routing demo last week. Before we talk next steps, tell me where you actually stand.

transcript.speaker: Tessa
transcript.text: Honestly, the quote is the problem. Forty per seat is more than we pay for half our stack combined.

transcript.speaker: Omar
transcript.text: Per seat it reads high, I agree. Across your nine dispatchers it is under a dollar per routed load. That is the number your CFO will ask about.

transcript.speaker: Tessa
transcript.text: Maybe. But Loadline quoted us thirty per seat last month. Why would I pay a third more for the same thing?

transcript.speaker: Omar
transcript.text: That thirty covers routing only. Tracking and API access are separate line items on their sheet. Priced like for like, the quotes land within five percent.

transcript.speaker: Tessa
transcript.text: Fine, say the price works. My team cannot absorb a six month rollout in the middle of peak season.

transcript.speaker: Omar
transcript.text: Six months is the figure for fleets over two hundred trucks. Beacon lands in the four week track, and dispatch keeps running through cutover.

transcript.speaker: Tessa
transcript.text: Four weeks. Who has that actually worked for?

transcript.speaker: Omar
transcript.text: Talon Cargo runs sixty trucks like you. They went live in nineteen days last quarter, and their dispatch lead logged the cutover day by day. I can send the log.

transcript.speaker: Tessa
transcript.text: Send it. But I am not signing anything before our budget freeze lifts in March.

transcript.speaker: Omar
transcript.text: Understood, so let us not talk contracts. Can I get thirty minutes with your dispatch lead next week to scope the four week plan against peak season?

transcript.speaker: Tessa
transcript.text: If the calendar allows it, yes. Send the Talon log first.

transcript.speaker: Omar
transcript.text: Both will be in your inbox today. Thanks for pushing on the numbers, Tessa.

suggestions.title: Price per routed load
suggestions.content: Reframe 40 per seat: across 9 dispatchers it is under 1 dollar per routed load at Beacon's volume.
suggestions.reason: The customer called the per seat price higher than half their stack combined.
suggestions.useEvidence: Omar reframed the price at 00:16: "it is under a dollar per routed load."

suggestions.title: Loadline quote breakdown
suggestions.content: The logged Loadline quote covers routing only. Tracking and API access are separate line items.
suggestions.reason: The customer cited a 30 per seat Loadline quote from last month.
suggestions.useEvidence: Omar priced the quotes like for like at 00:33: "the quotes land within five percent."

suggestions.title: Four week rollout track
suggestions.content: Six month rollouts apply to fleets over 200 trucks. Beacon's size fits the 4 week track with dispatch live throughout.
suggestions.reason: The customer said a six month rollout would not survive peak season.
suggestions.useEvidence: Omar corrected the rollout figure at 00:49: "Beacon lands in the four week track."

suggestions.title: Talon Cargo cutover log
suggestions.content: Talon Cargo, a 60 truck fleet, went live in 19 days last quarter. Offer their day by day cutover log.
suggestions.reason: The customer asked who the four week track had actually worked for.
suggestions.useEvidence: Omar cited the proof at 01:05: "went live in nineteen days last quarter."

suggestions.title: Offer a March price hold
suggestions.content: Offer to hold current pricing through the March budget freeze in exchange for a scoping call now.
suggestions.reason: The customer said nothing gets signed before the budget freeze lifts.
